title: Process creation BIFs — self, spawn, spawn_link
purpose: Running BEAM code cannot create new processes. This brief creates process_bifs.rs, extends ProcessContext with a spawn channel, and registers self/0, spawn/3, and spawn_link/3.
task: Create crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs.rs with three BIFs. Extend ProcessContext with a spawn facility. Register via register_gate2_bifs().
boundaries: This brief SHALL NOT implement spawn_monitor/3 (covered by combining spawn + monitor in later briefs)
This brief SHALL NOT implement named process registration (erlang:register/2)
This brief SHALL NOT implement spawn with options (erlang:spawn_opt/4)

requirements.title: erlang:self/0
requirements.spec: erlang:self/0 SHALL return the calling process PID as a Pid term. If pid is None, return Err(badarg).
acceptance: Result is a valid Pid term

requirements.title: erlang:spawn/3
requirements.spec: erlang:spawn/3 SHALL accept (Module, Function, Args), create a new process via ProcessContext spawn channel, set code position, load Args into X-registers, return new PID.
acceptance: spawn(Module, Function, []) returns a valid PID
spawn(Module, Function, [A, B]) creates process with x(0)=A, x(1)=B
Spawned process executes independently on scheduler

requirements.title: erlang:spawn_link/3 and BIF registration
requirements.spec: erlang:spawn_link/3 SHALL behave like spawn/3 but atomically establish a bidirectional link. All three BIFs registered via register_gate2_bifs(). ProcessContext extended without breaking Gate 1.
acceptance: spawn_link returns a valid PID with link established
Parent and child link sets contain each other
All 3 BIFs registered under erlang module
ProcessContext extension does not break Gate 1 BIFs
